archive_status: deferred (sweep tomorrow with other completed workorders)

handoff.reason: Plan authored as 8 read-only validation phases with hard stops at Phase 3 (after RAG reindex) and Phase 8 (final closure approval). Scope strictly post-merge validation of CODEREF-CORE; OPENCLAW rename explicitly excluded.

execution_rules.atomicity: Read-only validation. No commits. No code changes. The only file-modifying steps are RAG reindex (Phase 3) and snapshot regen (Phase 7); both are gated by hard stops.
execution_rules.rollback: If validation surfaces a regression: HALT, record in communication_log, do NOT modify code or snapshots, surface to user. User decides between fix workorder (out of scope) or known-issue stub.
execution_rules.service_management: Phase 3 requires the RAG server (port 52849) to be reachable. If it died from the cascade failure earlier in the session, restart it before proceeding.

hard_constraints: Read-only validation — no source code changes inside this workorder
Snapshot regen requires explicit user confirmation
If full test suite shows MORE failures than the post-merge baseline of 66, HALT and author a fix workorder
Do not touch OPENCLAW → automation rename — separate concern, addressed independently

plan_adjustments.summary: Phase 6 plan task assumes /data-source-report-use-case-analyzer consumes .coderef/semantic-registry.json. Skill actually consumes data-source reports at MIGRATION/REPORTS/data-research/{SUBJECT}/. No semantic-registry references in skill code. Phase 6 recorded as PASS-with-finding (input mismatch, not regression).

Phase 7 plan task assumes vitest __snapshots__/*.snap files pin index.json/semantic-registry.json contents. Codebase has zero __snapshots__ dirs and zero toMatchSnapshot calls in __tests__/src. Tests use direct JSON fixture comparison. Nothing to regenerate. Phase 7 PASS-by-vacuity.

communication_log.summary: Workorder authored fresh in CODEREF-CORE/coderef/workorder/ to validate the merged populate-relationships+headers fix (origin/main = fe6bdc9). Promoted from STUB-241. Four artifacts persisted; plan_type=phased; 8 phases; hard stops at 3 and 8.

Full vitest suite executed on HEAD=fe6bdc9 (main). Result: 80 test files (26 failed | 54 passed); 1340 tests (71 failed | 1269 passed). Baseline expected: 78 files (21 failed | 57 passed); 1340 tests (66 failed | 1274 passed). Delta: +5 failed files, +5 failed tests, -5 passed tests, +2 file count. Working tree NOT clean: 18 tracked test-fixture files show as DELETED (under __tests__/.test-integration-project/, __tests__/.test-venv-fixtures/, test-temp-cli/.coderef/). Likely cause: a prior test run mutated/cleaned these fixture files; current run sees them missing and tests dependent on them fail. This is environmental drift, not a regression introduced by the merged fix.

HALT per hard constraint: 'If full test suite shows MORE failures than the post-merge baseline of 66, HALT'. Surfacing to user. Recommended path: (a) restore deleted test fixtures via 'git checkout -- __tests__/.test-integration-project/ __tests__/.test-venv-fixtures/ test-temp-cli/' then re-run baseline; (b) if delta persists post-restore, author fix workorder for the new failures; (c) if delta resolves to 1274/66, resume Phase 2.
User confirmed Option A: cleanup-hook pattern recognized from prior session. Authorized fixture restore + re-run with expectation of 1274/66 baseline.

Ran 'git checkout -- __tests__/.test-integration-project/ __tests__/.test-venv-fixtures/ test-temp-cli/'. Re-ran full vitest. Result: 80 test files (23 failed | 57 passed); 1340 tests (66 failed | 1274 passed). Test count matches baseline EXACTLY. File-level count is +2 vs expected (23 vs 21) which is within ±2 flake tolerance and does not change total pass/fail counts. Phase 1 PASS. Advancing to Phase 2.

Initial sample of .coderef/index.json revealed STALE state: 2191 elements with usedBy[] count = 0, semantic-registry.json missing. index.generatedAt was 2026-04-28T22:40 (predates merged fix at c5b8f00 / fe6bdc9). Per plan task 'If missing or stale, run /coderef-pipeline ... to regenerate', invoked: node dist/src/cli/populate.js -v. Pipeline completed in 12.2s.

RAG server health: GET http://localhost:52849/api/health -> 200, status=healthy, version=1.0.0, uptime_s=14985, started_at=2026-05-01T15:24:37Z (PID 10820, ~4h uptime, survived earlier-session cascade). Pre-reindex vector store baseline: .coderef/coderef-vectors.json size=175 bytes, records={}, namespaces=[], dimension=1536, createdAt=2026-04-28T20:07:34Z, updatedAt=2026-04-28T20:07:36Z (store empty since 2026-04-28; never repopulated after morning's index regen). Confirms the silent-quality-regression risk identified in workorder context.

Ran 'node dist/src/cli/rag-index.js'. Duration: 1.44s pipeline + 2.7s wall. Files processed: 256. Chunks indexed: 0. Chunks skipped: 217. Chunks failed: 39. ALL 39 failures: 'Batch embedding failed: Invalid OpenAI API key'. Tokens used: 6169 (counted before API rejection). Vector store post-attempt: records still empty (0), updatedAt advanced to 2026-05-01T19:34:46Z. Chunk-conversion step (the failure mode that the populate-relationships fix addressed) ran cleanly — converter produced 39 batches successfully; failure was at OpenAI embedding call.

Validated OPENAI_API_KEY directly: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY' https://api.openai.com/v1/models -> HTTP 401, error.code='invalid_api_key', error.type='invalid_request_error', message confirms key 'sk-proj-...UnQA' is rejected by OpenAI. Key shape looks well-formed (164 chars, sk-proj prefix) — likely revoked, expired, or project-scoped restriction. This is environmental/credentials, NOT a code regression introduced by the merged fix. Phase 3 acceptance criteria 1 (server reachable) PASS; criterion 2 (vector index timestamp updated) PASS technically; criterion 3 (server health OK after) PASS; but business goal (post-fix embeddings actually exist on disk for downstream consumers) FAIL. The chunk-converter — the actual subject of the merged fix — produced 39 valid chunk batches without errors before hitting the credentials wall.

HARD STOP at Phase 3 boundary as planned. Surfacing to user. Decision needed: (a) refresh OPENAI_API_KEY and re-run rag-index — restores Phase 3 to true PASS and unblocks downstream skill spot-checks against real embeddings; (b) skip RAG re-index, accept that downstream phases will exercise skills against an empty vector store (Phase 4-6 skill behavior under empty embeddings is itself a meaningful test, but does not validate the populate-relationships fix end-to-end into RAG); (c) close workorder as conditional pass with a follow-up stub for credentials refresh + re-run.

User confirmed local Ollama-only setup. Directive: switch embedding provider to Ollama, confirm /api/tags reachable, identify embedding model, find provider-switch env vars, set CODEREF_LLM_PROVIDER=ollama + base URL + model, re-run reindex.

Ollama preflight: GET http://localhost:11434/api/tags -> 200, models include nomic-embed-text:latest (137M, F16). Reviewed src/cli/rag-index.ts and src/integration/llm/{ollama-provider.ts, model-registry.ts} — Ollama is fully supported. Resolved config: CODEREF_LLM_PROVIDER=ollama, CODEREF_LLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434 (default), CODEREF_LLM_API_KEY=ollama (default), embedding model=nomic-embed-text (default; dimensions=768 measured via 'ollama show'). Override via CODEREF_LLM_EMBED_MODEL if needed.

User-requested re-run of /close-workorder. Validator returned identical FAIL (closure_obligation_met + review_before_close on the same 27/30 pre-existing TRACKING/workorders.json rows). No state change — STUB-246 has not shipped yet, so the underlying drift is unchanged. Workorder remains at status=checkpoint_ready with closure_blocker=STUB-246. Confirms validator integrity: re-running close without addressing the blocker produces the same hard-stop, no false greens.

Workorder validation work is COMPLETE. Closure deferred. STUB-246 (tracking-workorders-closed-row-backfill, high priority, owner=ASSISTANT) authored to backfill closed_via + review_result on the 27/30 pre-validator-era closed rows in TRACKING/workorders.json. This workorder is parked at status=checkpoint_ready. closure_blocker=STUB-246. Re-run /close-workorder WO-CODEREF-CORE-POST-MERGE-SCANNER-VALIDATION-001 after STUB-246 ships and validator passes. Decision rationale: keep validation-workorder commit history clean; do not muddy audit trail by sweeping unrelated TRACKING ledger backfill into this workorder's commit. Two unrelated concerns get two separate workorders + audit trails.

/close-workorder Step 3 ran /validate-coderef-culture for CODEREF-CORE on 2026-05-01. Verdict: FAIL (2 fail-severity checks). FAIL reasons: (1) closure_obligation_met: 27 closed WOs in TRACKING/workorders.json missing 'closed_via' marker; (2) review_before_close: 30 closed WOs without 'review_result' field. The named WOs (close-workorder-closed-writer, coderef-core-skill-gap-remediation, coderef-semantic-integration-phase-4-5-fix, create-workorder-step-order-fix, dispatch-formatter-refactor, etc.) are PRE-EXISTING historical drift in the workorders ledger — NOT introduced by WO-CODEREF-CORE-POST-MERGE-SCANNER-VALIDATION-001. Per HARD-STOPS-AND-SOFT-STOPS, validator FAIL is a hard stop: close halts, status set to checkpoint_ready. Stub creation completed (STUB-243-245 + files), communication.json finalized through Phase 8 verdict. Workorder is functionally complete; closure is gated on TRACKING ledger backfill, not on the validation work itself. Decision required: (a) backfill closed_via + review_result on the 27/30 historical WOs in TRACKING/workorders.json (out of scope for this workorder), then re-run /close-workorder; (b) accept WARNING/FAIL state and force-close via --force-warning-ok (the FAIL is on closure_obligation_met which warning-mode does NOT cover — only WARNING-severity is covered by force-warning-ok); (c) leave at checkpoint_ready and address backfill in a separate workorder.

Final review compiled. Verdict proposal: CONDITIONAL PASS. Rationale: (1) Baseline tests match 1274/66 after fixture restore — merged fix introduced ZERO test regressions. (2) Scanner output verified rich — 2274 elements, 1486 with usedBy[], 2053 with imports[], 2059 with exports[]; sample element validated structurally. (3) RAG re-index successful with 256/256 chunks via Ollama — vector store rehydrated, server stable. (4) Three downstream skills validated: analyze-coderef-semantics ran without throwing (degraded mode due to missing foundation-docs export tree); coderef-analyze procedure ran end-to-end producing grounded analysis; data-source-report-use-case-analyzer proven not-a-consumer-of-scanner-output (plan defect). (5) No snapshot regen needed (repo doesn't use snapshot testing for scanner output). The merged populate-relationships+headers fix is functioning as intended on the scanner side. Findings collected for follow-up stub consideration listed below.

follow_up_stub_candidates.title: RAG chunk-converter element-level enrichment
follow_up_stub_candidates.rationale: Phase 3 finding: vector chunks emit dependencyCount=0/dependentCount=0 even though index.json has 1486 elements with usedBy[]. Either (a) ratify file-level chunking as by-design and document, or (b) extend chunk-converter to include element-level chunks with relationship metadata.

follow_up_stub_candidates.title: Generate foundation-docs export pipeline outputs in CODEREF-CORE post-populate
follow_up_stub_candidates.rationale: Phase 4 finding: analyze-coderef-semantics expects .coderef/exports/foundation-docs/, .coderef/header-index.json, manifest.json — none exist post-populate. Either run scripts/doc-gen/* in CODEREF-CORE post-populate as part of canonical close-out, or document the skill's degraded mode as the expected mid-pipeline behavior.

follow_up_stub_candidates.title: Phase-6-of-this-plan defect: replan downstream consumer set
follow_up_stub_candidates.rationale: Phase 6 plan task assumed /data-source-report-use-case-analyzer is the heaviest semantic-registry consumer. It is not — it consumes data-source reports. Future post-merge validation workorders should identify actual scanner-output consumers (e.g., coderef-analyze, drift-generator, validation-generator, RAG chunk-converter) — not meta-analysis tools.

communication_log.summary: Phase 7 task: 'find -path "*/__snapshots__/*.snap" + grep snapshot files for index.json/semantic-registry.json references; surface diffs to user.' Investigation: zero __snapshots__ directories anywhere in CODEREF-CORE (excluding node_modules), zero toMatchSnapshot/toMatchInlineSnapshot calls in __tests__ or src. The repo does NOT use vitest's snapshot testing for scanner output. Tests that reference index.json (errors.test.ts, fileGeneration-integration.test.ts, drift-generator.test.ts, index-generator.test.ts, pipeline-snapshot.test.ts, populate-cli.test.ts, semantic-canonical.test.ts) compare via direct fixture/JSON parsing, not vitest .snap files. No snapshots exist to regenerate. Phase 7 has zero work to do. Plan adjustment ADJ-02 recorded. Phase 7 PASS-by-vacuity.

Phase 6 plan task: 'Verify /data-source-report-use-case-analyzer consumes the populated semantic-registry.json without errors.' Investigation finding: the skill does NOT consume .coderef/semantic-registry.json. Per its SKILL.md, this skill consumes a data-source report at 'MIGRATION/REPORTS/data-research/{SUBJECT}/{SUBJECT}-DATA-SOURCE-REPORT.md' (a meta-analysis output), not scanner output. grep across the skill directory for 'semantic-registry' returns zero matches. Furthermore, MIGRATION/REPORTS/data-research/ does not exist on disk. The plan's framing ('heaviest semantic-registry consumer') is a plan defect — this skill is unrelated to scanner-side semantic registry data. Phase 6 cannot be validated as written. PLAN ADJUSTMENT: ADJ-01 — phase_6 task description does not match skill capability; recording as PASS-with-finding because the skill's true inputs (data-source reports) are not gated by the merged scanner fix. The merged fix's downstream validation does not depend on this skill. Recommend Phase 8 verdict include this as a documented plan defect rather than a regression.

coderef-analyze is a procedural read-only artifact-consuming skill (no CLI). Exercised by running its 5-step procedure: (1) resolved target = CODEREF-CORE, (2) verified required inputs (.coderef/index.json, .coderef/graph.json) present + optional reports all parseable, (3) read artifacts, (4) produced grounded inventory analysis. Findings from the post-fix scanner output: 2274 elements (method:1059, function:668, interface:376, class:118, type:39, constant:12, component:2); graph 2274 nodes / 11671 edges; relationships 1486 with usedBy[], 2053 with imports[], 2059 with exports[]; patterns asyncPatterns:267 / middleware:39 / errorHandling:798 / decorators:0; coverage 35% (82/235 tested); drift 0% (no stale files); validation 4 broken @coderef references (3 in test fixtures using deliberately-missing example.ts:alpha/missing, 1 in src/pipeline/generators/validation-generator.ts:62 referencing literal placeholder '@coderef{file:element}'); complexity summary present with totalElements/averageComplexity/averageLOC/highComplexityCount/elements keys. Skill procedure ran end-to-end without throwing. Phase 5 PASS.

Ran 'python cli.py --dispatch-seed --project-root <coderef-core> --task-hint-json {layer:service, capability:semantic-relationship-population, keywords:[usedBy,imports,relationships]}'. Skill completed without exception in 2251ms. Returned structured JSON with wiring_status='degraded', fallback_used=true. Output is valid JSON. Phase 4 success criteria (skill completes without throwing; report is valid JSON) MET. PASS.

downstream_gap_finding.title: analyze-coderef-semantics is degraded due to missing semantic export artifacts
skill_init_errors: manifest.json not found (provenance fail across manifest_integrity, artifact_freshness, input_availability)
downstream_gap_finding.interpretation: The merged populate-relationships fix correctly populates index.json (1486 elements with usedBy[] verified Phase 2). But this analysis skill expects sibling artifacts — foundation-docs export tree, header-index.json, manifest.json — that the populate pipeline did NOT produce. Headers were likely never written via --source-headers (off by default per workorder hard constraint 'Headers are optional and off by default'). Foundation-docs export pipeline is a SEPARATE infrastructure (scripts/doc-gen/) that needs to run after populate. Skill behavior is graceful (returns structured fallback, no crash) which is the actual Phase 4 acceptance criterion.
downstream_gap_finding.phase_8_consideration: Workorder dispatched skill without first running --source-headers + foundation-docs generators. Either (a) running the skill against this state IS the realistic test (degraded mode is what consumers will hit if they call the skill mid-pipeline) — Phase 4 PASS as recorded; or (b) follow-up stub for 'run --source-headers + scripts/doc-gen/* in CODEREF-CORE then re-run analyze-coderef-semantics for full-mode validation'. The merged fix's specific deliverable (scanner relationship population) is intact — this gap is in adjacent semantic-export infrastructure.

communication_log.summary: FINDING: Sample queries against the new vector store return file-level chunks with metadata showing dependencyCount=0 / dependentCount=0 for every chunk inspected (e.g., src/export/index.ts, src/integration/index.ts). The merged populate-relationships fix DID populate .coderef/index.json correctly (1486 elements with usedBy[]) — verified directly in Phase 2. But the RAG chunk-converter is emitting file-level chunks WITHOUT consuming the rich relationship counts into chunk metadata. So usedBy[]/imports[] data is searchable via direct index.json queries but NOT via embedded vector search. Two interpretations: (a) BY-DESIGN: chunk-converter is intentionally file-level only; semantic relationship queries should target index.json directly. (b) GAP: chunk-converter should include relationship counts in metadata (or emit element-level chunks). Workorder's plan does NOT explicitly require RAG chunks to carry usedBy/imports — only that 'RAG vector index timestamp updated' and 'health OK'. Phase 3 acceptance criteria are technically MET (server, timestamp, health). Recording the finding for Phase 8 verdict; if interpretation (b) is correct, this would be a separate stub not a Phase-3 fail.

Re-ran rag-index with --provider ollama. First attempt failed with VectorStoreError dimension mismatch (1536 OpenAI vs 768 Ollama); CLI suggested --reset. Second run with --reset succeeded. Pipeline duration: 725.63s (~12 min for ~256 chunks; Ollama embeddings are single-text-per-call, not batched). Files processed: 256. Chunks indexed: 256. Chunks failed: 0. Vector store post-reindex: dimension=768, records=256, updatedAt=2026-05-01T20:05:52Z. RAG server health post-reindex: 200 OK, uptime_s=16884 (still PID 10820, no crash). Sample query 'populate semantic relationships usedBy reverse dependency resolver' returned src/registry/types.ts as top hit at 44.8% — relevant to the merged-fix subject area. Phase 3 PASS.

Post-regen index.json: 2274 elements (expected ~2283; within tolerance), 2059 with exports[] (expected ~2053; match), 1486 with usedBy[] (expected ~1486; EXACT match), 1486 candidates with both. Sample element 'MockEnvironment' (interface, __tests__/generators/helpers.ts:8) shows populated exports[5], usedBy[1], imports[6] as structured objects. semantic-registry.json regenerated at 19:11:38 with version=1.0.0, generated_from='.coderef/index.json', 2274 entries — mirror confirmed. Phase 2 PASS.

WO-TRACKING-WORKORDERS-CLOSED-ROW-BACKFILL-001 (commits 2936646 + 07ccde6) backfilled both fields using a two-bucket sentinel scheme (reconstructed_from_communication_log / unrecoverable_pre_validator). Closed via /close-workorder --no-archive. Phase 1-8 verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS (Phase 1 baseline 1274/66 match, Phase 2 scanner-output verified rich, Phase 3 RAG reindex 256/256 chunks via Ollama, Phases 4-7 analysis spot-checks PASS or PASS-with-finding, Phase 8 verdict). Three follow-up findings recorded as stub candidates (not blockers): (1) RAG chunk-converter element-level enrichment, (2) foundation-docs export pipeline post-populate, (3) Phase-6 plan defect for future validation WOs.
